_theme_labels.T7: AI in the Enterprise: Leadership & Verticals

subtheme_focus._note: Within the core theme T1, prioritize these subclusters (read membership from clusters.json). De-emphasize the rest.

time_constraints: Evenings light: keep after ~17:00 open for networking / parties / hallway track
Attending all four days including the Workshop Day 2026-06-28

_objective_weights_basis: User chose 'paced' over 'packed' (pacing up, raw interest tempered), serendipity 'fit when cheap' (modest 0.12), focused interests (breadth moderate not high). Weights are the user's, derived from their choices.
hard_constraints: Paced, not packed - protect breaks; a real break beats six scattered gaps
Pitch most sessions deep / advanced & hands-on
Anchor the best few main-stage keynotes (marquee + in-lane) even over a track talk
Include a couple of standout hands-on workshops, but not workshop-heavy
Attend all four days incl. the Workshop Day (Jun 28)
_forced_in_basis: These are my read of 'anchor the best few keynotes': the marquee OpenAI opening keynote + the three most in-lane keynotes (Dex Horthy on harness engineering, Tariq Shaukat on agent verification/evals, Matt Pocock on building agent skills/context engineering). User can swap any.

serendipity_candidates.tension: 17:00-18:00 falls in the evenings-light window; include only as a gentle close to Workshop Day if it doesn't fight the evening preference
